subject: Mathematics
title: River Meanders and Pi
description: A micro-lesson that makes River Meanders and Pi usable.

hook.text: Rivers don't flow in straight lines. They curve, loop, and wander across the landscape.

buildup.text: The ratio of a river's actual length to its straight-line distance tends to average around 3.14.

discovery.text: This ratio, close to pi, arises from the physics of erosion and sediment deposit.

twist.text: It's not exact. The pi connection is an average tendency, not a law. But it's a striking coincidence.

climax.text: Small disturbances in flow amplify into curves through positive feedback—chaos at landscape scale.

punchline.text: Rivers wander by about pi. Nature loves that number.

quiz.question: What ratio do river meanders tend to approximate?
